name: Skill audit tracking broken
purpose: Documents that the skill-audit skill's state-file tracking is incomplete — only 5 audit state files persist despite many more skills being audited, audit history is being lost.
notes: No frontmatter originSessionId. No canonical counterpart. SoNash-specific: references skill-audit skill state files at .claude/state/task-skill-audit-{name}.state.json. Lists 5 known persisted: alerts, pr-retro, create-audit, session-begin, skill-audit. The underlying problem (audit tracking mechanism losing history) is a SoNash-internal infrastructure gap. Not present in JASON-OS user-home. Only portable principle: if a project has a skill-audit skill, verify its state-file tracking mechanism persists correctly.
portable_elements: Principle that audit tracking state files should be validated is portable as a methodology note for any skill-audit implementation.

name: SonarCloud fewer findings investigation
purpose: Tracks investigation into why SonarCloud hotspot/issue count dropped during PR reviews — workflow disable ruled out, root cause still open as of 2026-03-25.
notes: No frontmatter originSessionId. No canonical counterpart. SoNash-specific: references SonarCloud org, project key, sonar-project.properties exclusions (node_modules, dist, .next, coverage, .planning, scripts/archive), baseline 778 issues. Investigation started 2026-03-25, status 'still to investigate'. Content-bleed risk in project-specific config details. Not present in JASON-OS (JASON-OS SonarCloud disabled entirely per project_sonarcloud_disabled.md in JASON-OS memory).
portable_elements: Pattern: automatic SonarCloud analysis can diverge from workflow-based analysis; investigate sonar-project.properties exclusions before assuming genuine improvement. Applicable to any SonarCloud-enabled project.

name: SoNash project identity
purpose: Clarifies that SoNash is a privacy-first recovery notebook app for the sobriety community — NOT a health tracker — and that Claude Code automation is infrastructure within the app, not the product itself.
notes: No frontmatter originSessionId. No canonical counterpart. Entirely SoNash-specific — defines the product identity of the SoNash app itself. Not present in JASON-OS. Not-portable as a unit; only meta-pattern is portable: each project should have an identity memory clarifying product vs tooling framing.
portable_elements: Meta-pattern: maintain a project identity memory that separates what the project IS from what tooling infrastructure is built within it. Prevents conflation errors across sessions.

name: Pre-push hook skip variables
purpose: Reference table for SoNash pre-push hook SKIP_ environment variables — 6 independent gates with distinct vars, SKIP_REASON required on all, 'pre-existing' wording rejected.
sanitize_fields: SKIP_REVIEWER gate reference (SoNash-specific code-reviewer coverage gate)
6 specific SKIP_ var names (may differ in JASON-OS)
notes: No frontmatter originSessionId. No canonical counterpart. SoNash-specific: the 6 SKIP_ vars (SKIP_WARNINGS, SKIP_PROPAGATION, SKIP_CC, SKIP_COG_CC, SKIP_TRIGGERS, SKIP_REVIEWER) are defined in SoNash's pre-push hook. JASON-OS has a different hook architecture (block-push-to-main.js). The 'no pre-existing in SKIP_REASON' rule and the three resolution options are portable principles. Content-bleed risk on specific var names. JASON-OS counterpart reference_pre_push_skip_vars.md covers similar but different vars.
portable_elements: 'No pre-existing in SKIP_REASON' principle is universally applicable. Three resolution options pattern (baseline-it, reference debt tracker, fix it) is portable to any skip-gate system. SKIP_REASON required on all skips is a universally sound practice.

name: Custom statusline architecture
purpose: Technical reference for SoNash custom Go statusline — binary location, config files, cache paths, widget layout, v3 changes, API-backed widget details, and operational constraints.
sanitize_fields: cache path (~/.claude/statusline/cache/ — shared but project-specific naming)
notes: frontmatter originSessionId: 7389d098-40f3-498d-a495-fe6dd68bdf2c. No canonical counterpart. SoNash-specific versioned binary (sonash-statusline-v3.exe). Cross-references feedback_statusline_rebuild_safety.md. V3 changes documented (FlexModel for Claude Code >=2.1.89, isEntryUnacked filter). Cache files listed (weather.json, github-pr.json, github-ci.json, backoff.json). Notable: go1.26.1 required (go1.23.6 produces broken binaries) — critical operational constraint. Partially portable: JASON-OS already ported the statusline to jason-statusline-v2.exe. This memory provides the authoritative SoNash design reference for that port.
portable_elements: FlexModel field handling (string vs object for model field), isEntryUnacked filter logic, weather forecast API date filtering, backoff.json cache pattern — all portable to any Go statusline implementation. Go version constraint note is portable operational guidance.

portable_elements: Overwrite-hazard documentation pattern (any script that regenerates a canonical file must also update upstream source) is a portable architecture principle. The pipeline ordering (intake -> dedup -> roadmap-refs -> views) is a portable debt-management architecture template.
